Transcrição Completa: 7 QA 4
Conversa
[00:00]
"Hey. Uh, all right. First question. Can you explain a SWAT analysis like I'm five?"
[00:05]
"Yeah. Uh, SWAT analysis is an acronym. Strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats."
[00:12]
"So, when you're looking at the competitors, what are they strong at? Right? Do they have more people? Do they have a better offering? Is their pricing better than yours? Are they um can they deliver faster than you? That's a strength, right?"
[00:26]
"What is a weakness? Maybe they're too big and they can't move as fast as you can and that's a vulnerability that you could attack. That would be a weakness, right?"
[00:36]
"Um opportunity could be um maybe they don't have a certain type of social media or a new digital channel that you can capitalize on that they're not thinking about and that can help you know be more valuable to clients. That could be an opportunity you could snap into. Right?"
[00:52]
"A threat could be um an external threat. It could be something going on with the economy or it could be um a competitor specifically that they just built a brand new beautiful office, you know, a mile and a half from your current office and you're afraid they're going to steal your employees."
[01:10]
"Got it. Um is there anything that you recommend, [clears throat and cough] for example, secret shopping your competitors to find some of this information out besides Google and like what you can find online?"
[01:21]
"100%. I mean, you know, there there's ethic um ethics in there that you got to have your own moral compass, but you know, I in that document, there's a bunch of examples of of places to go and look at it. But I personally think you should secret shop."
[01:35]
"I mean, you know, get out there, understand what are they pitching, call previous clients that used to be with them, call current clients, right? I mean, you're a profit business. You're wanting to win. Do the things nobody else wants to do."
[01:47]
"Would you go as far like let's use the example you're going for a multi-10 million dollar plus exit. I mean would you go as far as like developing relationships with their staff to try to find out some of these answers or like how how far do you really go to get some of this stuff?"
[02:04]
"Yeah, listen this this business is all service based, right? You don't have a widget or a product. People are the product and if you want to win at the highest level, you got to get great people and you got to do whatever you need to do to get those great people. So build a badass culture and and steal talent."
[02:23]
"What What is the best way to get competitors? a key staff"
[02:28]
"um to get competitors. Key staff is and and we're going to jump into it, but you got to build a freaking amazing culture, right? You got to, you know, benchmarking baseline stuff is is pay and um you know, cool office, but you know, what are the benefits? What are the perks? How do you treat them?"
[02:46]
"You know, do they feel like they have purpose? Are they aligned? You know, do they want to come to work? Right? You know, you've got to build an amazing culture to retain amazing talent."
[02:57]
"Nice. And then um last last question, what's the best way to get competitors clients? Like how do you actually find out like who your competitors are working with?"
[03:06]
"So there's a place called uh Windmo. We'll put it down here in the notes. Um it's a resource for agencies to do prospecting for for companies. And sometimes they'll actually be able to pull the data to show the current agency they're working with."
[03:21]
"So, not only the decision maker of um of the the client you're going after, but even potentially the agency they're using."
[03:29]
"Good."
[03:30]
"Cool. All right, guys. Again, we're wrapping up that. Ask us more questions. We'll get them answered u in the Facebook group and on the platform. I'll see you in the next section."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Multi-10 million dollar+ exit mencionado como exemplo de objetivo
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Análise SWOT: Framework para avaliar concorrentes (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats)
Secret Shopping: Estratégia de inteligência competitiva para entender pitch e ofertas dos concorrentes
Pesquisa de Clientes: Contatar clientes anteriores e atuais dos concorrentes
Roubo de Talentos: Estratégia ativa de recrutamento de funcionários-chave dos concorrentes
Windmo: Ferramenta de prospecção para identificar clientes e agências concorrentes
ESTRUTURA:
Análise de Forças: Mais pessoas, melhor oferta, preço melhor, entrega mais rápida
Análise de Fraquezas: Tamanho excessivo, lentidão operacional
Análise de Oportunidades: Canais digitais inexplorados, redes sociais não utilizadas
Análise de Ameaças: Fatores econômicos, novos escritórios concorrentes, risco de perda de funcionários
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Negócio baseado em serviços (sem produto físico)
Foco em lucro e vitória competitiva
Objetivo de saída multi-milhonária
DURAÇÃO: ~3:40 minutos FORMATO: Sessão de perguntas e respostas (Q&A) educacional sobre análise competitiva e estratégias de crescimento de agência
